hypothesis: Continuous soul scoring relative to corpus mean will eliminate dead zone and route work away from meta toward high-benefit domains
method: Replace threshold-based soul boost (>1.5 boost, <0.5 penalty, 0.5-1.5 dead zone) with continuous scoring: delta = domain_ratio - corpus_mean_ratio, boost = delta * 0.15 clamped to [-0.4, +0.8]

pre_registration.prediction: Meta domain drops in dispatch ranking; non-meta high-benefit domains rise
pre_registration.falsification: Meta rank unchanged or increases despite continuous scoring
pre_registration.quantitative: Meta soul_boost changes from 0 to negative; >5 domains shift rank

results.mechanism: Weighted corpus mean (2.73) replaces fixed thresholds. Meta (1.34x) now penalized as below-average. Old dead zone 0.5-1.5 had 7 domains with zero scoring.

verification_s519.notes: Soul-benefit was already wired into UCB1 score at dispatch_scoring.py lines 336-350. Meta gets -0.208 penalty (ratio 1.34x vs corpus mean 2.73x). Top human-benefit domains get up to +0.8 boost. 17 qualified domains scored continuously. No code change needed — wiring predates this verification.
